### 1. Why water is not a "cure"
The common cold is caused by viruses (most commonly rhinoviruses). Because the infection is viral and not bacterial, antibiotics do not work. Once the virus enters your cells and begins to replicate, your body initiates an inflammatory response (producing symptoms like fever, mucus, and congestion). 

Drinking water does not have the ability to kill the virus or stop its replication. The "cure" is simply the passage of time as your immune system completes its job.
